essence: Shapes, levels, and finishes concrete surfaces to create strong, durable foundations and structures.

identity.title: Concrete Finisher
identity.summary: Pours, levels, smooths, and finishes concrete for floors, pavements, foundations, and structural elements, ensuring strength, durability, and a clean final surface.

salary.notes: Large commercial projects, infrastructure work, and specialist finishing (e.g., power‑floating, decorative concrete) can exceed £50k-£60k+.

qualifications: Apprenticeship or Level 2/3 Diploma in Concrete Occupations or Construction
Knowledge of concrete mixes, curing, and finishing techniques
Ability to use trowels, screeds, floats, and power‑floating equipment
Strong physical fitness and ability to work outdoors

lifestyle.description: Concrete finishers work on construction sites preparing, pouring, and finishing concrete for foundations, floors, pavements, and structural elements. Days involve setting forms, levelling surfaces, smoothing and texturing concrete, and ensuring proper curing. The role is physical, outdoors, and often time‑sensitive due to concrete setting windows. This lifestyle suits individuals who enjoy hands‑on work, teamwork, and contributing to the structural backbone of buildings and infrastructure.

habits: Levels and smooths concrete with precision
Keeps tools clean and ready for fast‑paced work
